body:
This PR updates the discussion about coordinate systems in the physics documentation.

Although, I think this discussion should be made (and in greater detail) in the `Grids` tutorial. But this page is out of date so it's worth updating it (if we want to keep the physics documentation...)

Closes #1779

Note, I also changed the title of this page so that it does not refer to "notation". I think we want to have a notation page, but it should serve a different purpose and cover both math and unicode symbols, similar to the notation page for Breeze: https://numericalearth.github.io/BreezeDocumentation/stable/appendix/notation/

More generally I think we should emphasize code documentation, and include equations interspersed with code, rather than having "physics" docs that are a bit divorced from the code and numerics.
title: Update coordinate system information in physics documentation

body:
Hello Oceananigans community, I am fairly new here and I am interested in building my simulations and contribution skills with the Oceananigans package. I have opened a discussion post [here](https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/discussions/4794) and an issue [here](https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/issues/4898), with idea of implementing a basic lock-exchange problem. My goal is to use this case to deepen my understanding of computational fluid dynamics, boundary conditions, and density-driven mixing. My ideal outcome is to produce a fully documented example script that meets the standards of the existing examples. 

I have opened this PR with a very basic version which includes my current understanding of how to run the simulations. I will happily welcome any and all feedback for any changes or ideas that would help me build on this existing script to make it a worthwhile contribution. So far, I have pulled from the [internal tide example](https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/blob/main/examples/internal_tide.jl) as well as the [lock release validation case](https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/blob/main/validation/z_star_coordinate/lock_release.jl), and I will continue to build on what I have so far. 

Thank you in advance for your time and help!
title: Lock Exchange Example
